notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 4 evals (17/17 assertions). Without-skill achieves 29.4% (7/17).
The skill's strongest advantage is in scope discrimination — negative evals (3 and 4) show 100% vs 0% pass rate. Without the skill, Claude has no concept of when NOT to write a PRD.
For positive evals, the skill enforces structural completeness (10-section template, MoSCoW, named personas, failure thresholds) that Claude doesn't naturally produce.

Without-skill output quality is actually decent on eval 2 (80% pass rate) — Claude naturally includes scope boundaries, dependencies, and AI-specific risks for complex topics. The skill's added value is in MoSCoW enforcement and template consistency.
Timing and token data not captured in this run (JSON parsing failed on CLI output). Future runs should use a more robust output capture method.
